reviews.name: Sherry
reviews.text: Service was great.Food was sooo delicious. Music a tad too loud but we were seated right under a speaker. Authentic Mexican food in a great atmosphere.

reviews.name: Mjf 1240
reviews.text: El Molino has an extremely endearing atmosphere, from the beautiful portraits of angels and the Virgin Mary which feels like home for many Hispanics. To the wonderful music playing over their speakers that feels like Saturday morning cleaning with your mother. The food itself certainly was authentic, very filling and cooked perfectly. Staff was extremely friendly and attentive, ensuring that the experience was as pleasant as the decor around me. Definitely a must visit if you’ve never been here before. My friend loved the margaritas.

reviews.name: Erika Medina
reviews.text: I got fajitas and a mango margarita it was delicious. Food was hot our server was really nice. Not to mention the margaritas were amazing. It has a great atmosphere and looks very lovely from the inside. I think the renovations look very elegant

reviews.name: Isidro Guzman
reviews.text: It was a great experience and amazing food and staff are very helpful

reviews.name: Gregorio Villegas
reviews.text: Excellent service and delicious food, 10/10. I highly recommend it. Ask for Daniel; he's very friendly and provides excellent service. Clean and beautiful restaurant.

reviews.name: Stephanie Arreguin
reviews.text: Excellent food, our waiter Daniel was very friendly. The restaurant is very clean and nice, and the service was great. Everything was delicious, 10/10, I highly recommend it.

reviews.name: Ajit Kaur
reviews.text: the waiter was kind and cooperative

reviews.name: Alex Czerchlanski
reviews.text: "The food was authentic, delicious, and plentiful. Daniel, our waiter, was: attentive, friendly, and provided a great first time experience. We look forward to going back soon! Great job service team!"

reviews.name: Kate Christerson
reviews.text: It's been awhile since enjoying a meal at El Molino. The atmosphere has changed from what used to be a more cantina vibe to an upscale restaurant. Dinner was delicious, had the Michaca Chimmichanga! The husband had tacos, with home made fresh corn tortillas, I thought he licked the plate thats how clean it was. Will definitely come back in the future.

reviews.name: Kelsey Kozar
reviews.text: Went to celebrate my birthday with a friend. Daniel was an awesome server. He checked on us and brought us everything we asked for in a timely manner. The food was great, especially the enchiladas! Had such a great time celebrating!

reviews.name: Camille Buda
reviews.text: Great environment and daniel was amazing!!

reviews.name: Grace Hersh
reviews.text: Have been going to El Molino for almost 30 years before they closed to "remodel". Went to check it out. I am pretty sure they are under new management after the experience. There's some new paint and they reupholstered, but while the cosmetic changes are okay to good, everything substantial is dismal now. The food is not the same and it's definitely not as good as under previous management. The chimichanga wasn't crispy, it was soggy. They put the lettuce and sour cream inside so you didn't have options. And it just doesn't taste as good. The service was ridiculously slow. It took almost an hour to get 2 chimichangas. They don't check in on you while you're waiting. After the food was dropped, no check in for the 3-bite rule. Getting the waiter to come back was a job because anytime he came by, he never even looked our way in multiple passes. It took 40 minutes to get the check. And then when we did get it, the total was a ridiculous 61 dollars and change for 2 glasses of water and 2 chimichangas! The menu shows the price of a chimichanga dinner at $16. Add $2 for Suizas. For the choices of fillings it says, same options as the Burrito which is listed right above it in the menu. There was a large choice of fillings including "steak" and "skirt steak"along with chicken, carnitas, Al pastor, Machaca beef, etc. We ordered the skirt steak filling. There is no additional pricing on the menu, no mention from the waiter when we ordered either. But that's where 2 waters and 2 chimichangas turned into $62! They charged us $8 per plate for the skirt steak. I immediately got up to ask for a manager. Some young guy came and kept insisting... Did you order the steak or the arrachera (skirt steak fajitas)? I said we did order the skirt steak. He said, well that's special option, we don't serve that unless you special ordered it. I said it's in the menu. He argued with me that it's not a regular option. He said that's why it's an upcharge. I insisted it's on the menu as a regular option with no mention of an upcharge. The Suizas showed the upcharge on the menu. All the meat options are listed one after another under the burritos. The chimichanga says, "Same options as the burrito". This young man kept insisting that I didn't read the menu correctly and the don't offer the skirt steak as a regular option. I finally told him to stop arguing with me and get a menu. And sure enough, there it is plain as day on the menu. And he said, well the menus are new. It should have been listed differently. If you didn't properly proofread your menu before okaying the final printing, that doesn't mean you can charge me whatever you see fit. He kept insisting on the upcharge. If we had been told it was $8 extra per plate, I'm sure we wouldn't have ordered it or been surprised at the cost of the bill. That surcharge is half the price of the entire dinner. That's usurious!!! The waiter had the surcharges removed by an older gentleman while this young guy( I'm pretty sure he's the son of the new owner who clearly has zero idea how to run a business and treat a customer, ESPECIALLY WHEN HE HAD NO IDEA WHAT HE WAS TAKING ABOUT AND WAS 100 PERCENT IN THE WRONG BOTH FOR ARGUING WITH THE CUSTOMER AND WRITING ABOUT THE MENU HERE), kept arguing with me. The older gentleman came up after I paid the bill and laid the same lame excuse about these being new menus. Will NEVER be going back. I'll be surprised if they survive a year in business. Dismal service, dismal treatment, poor management and mediocre food. ZERO stars... Do NOT RECOMMEND anymore. Used to be one of my fave places. NEW BUT DEFINITELY NOT IMPROVED. 🤬👎👎👎

reviews.name: Monica Lagunes
reviews.text: First time at this restaurant and it was great! Very nice for a date night or just going for a drink at the bar. Drinks are just right and the food is wonderful. Prices are reasonable not to bad. Host is very nice and waiters are as nice too. Really enjoyed the live calming music on Saturday night and also enjoyed every single song after that. Highly recommend!!

reviews.name: Olivia Gustafson
reviews.text: First time back since the renovation and WOW! The food was already top notch before but today’s experience was next level. The interior is beautifully redone, very tasteful with great seating. I had three tacos, pork, carne asada, and al pastor, and my boyfriend got the steak quesadilla. The salsas they bring out are to die for, the tortillas are homemade, and the meats were incredible. We skipped drinks this time but definitely won’t next time, the options looked so good. Our waiter was fantastic too, very attentive and gave great recommendations. On top of everything, the prices were very fair. We will absolutely be back soon and I cannot wait!!

reviews.name: tim martin
reviews.text: Great food!! Service top notch! Always good

reviews.name: Marisol Dominguez Hernandez
reviews.text: Excellent service!!! Delicious food!!!

reviews.name: Arath Hernandez
reviews.text: A place with delicious food, excellent service, and a wonderful atmosphere.

reviews.name: Kelly Hernandez-Jusino
reviews.text: Food was perfect! service was amazing!

reviews.name: George Papadakis
reviews.text: Real disappointment. They apparently think it's legal to charge more than the listed item pricing. State and local laws usually require restaurants to honor the posted or advertised price, often enforced by the Department of Consumer Affairs or Weights and Measures. While service charges or mandatory gratuities are legal, they should ideally be disclosed, especially in areas with strict transparent pricing laws. El Molino doesn't disclose and clearly decides what they want to charge after the ordering.

reviews.name: Laura Ruiz
reviews.text: We had dinner here yesterday, and we have found the best mexican food in town. Service is awesome and the margaritas and tequila are 10/10! They make their own fresh tortillas every morning. You can tell they are home made! Highly recommend their shrimp and papas fritas. Gracias Juan, por hacernos sentir como en casa😊. Hasta pronto!

reviews.name: Jeff Bomer (Boomer)
reviews.text: The steak was great! The nachos SUCK! They had lettuce on them as well. You would think a mexican resturant could get Nachos right.

reviews.name: Susan Grossenbach
reviews.text: Very beautifully remodel. But even better, the authentic delicious food.

reviews.name: Suahilee Santana
reviews.text: Ordered nachos el molino & was very disappointed the meat had nothing but fat & was very chewy. Guacamole had no flavor at all. the price they charged me was not worthy at all. Very, very disappointed

reviews.name: Mark Obrien
reviews.text: What a find! My wife and I live in Chicago, and our friends live in Huntley. We had a taste for Mexican and El Molino didn't disappoint. Every aspect of this restaurant was fantastic. A beautiful looking restaurant, easy parking, beautiful bar areas and a wonderful staff. We enjoyed both breakfast and lunch because we went on a Saturday and both menus were offered. Everything we had was so tasty. If you want a Bloody Mary, the bar tender knows how to make a great one! We will definitely be back! Well worth the trip!

reviews.name: George Chris
reviews.text: "WOW! WOW! WOW! This was a scam we’ve never seen. Been coming to el molino for at least 30 years. First time since new owners opened it in November. We were received warmly. Food was good. Service also very good. But then the bill came and they had padded on an additional 10-20% on several items. Increased the total almost $25. On a party of 4. Figured it had to be a mistake, right? Nope, instead the waitress insisted that the small print at the bottom of the menu allows them to change the prices on the menu at their discretion. We asked for the manager who repeatedly argued that the menu prices didn’t reflect what the price could be. Finally after repeated requests the bill was corrected. Please note: None of our meals were marked MP. Nowhere in the menu is it clear that prices may not be what is printed unless you happen to look for the fine print below allergies, etc. And never have we had to fight to pay what was clearly marked for all. Imagine if this was how it was everywhere you shopped and ate. Ridiculous. Strongly suggest avoiding El Molino or at the very least verifying the prices shown are what you’ll be getting charged…before you order."

reviews.name: Stephanie Doubek
reviews.text: Food was barely warm, too much mole on the enchiladas. The beef enchiladas was not edible, the mole chicken was just wings and legs, so just bones in sauce. Bartender was cordial, but not friendly or a bartender. Sadly, after 25+ years, with so many happy memories, this was a terrible experience. I don’t think we will be back. And 8$ for draft Pacifico and Dos XX? That is expensive and not the advertised price. The hostess was a delight! Keep her!

reviews.name: Richard Beech
reviews.text: It's good Mexican food. They did seem to nickel and dime me for items like sour cream and tortilla shells. Don't know if I would recommend. They also added a 3% charge for miscellaneous. If you can put up with that it's good

reviews.name: Randy Harbin
reviews.text: The place was packed so we ate at the bar. Niagara Modelo on tap! Chips and salsa were outstanding. Very spicy red sauce just the way I like it. I had the carne asada with the Chipotle sauce. The steak was done perfectly as ordered. Best Mexican food I've had in a while. We will be back!

reviews.name: Kristi Harbin
reviews.text: 1st time here busy place. Sat at the bar. Variety on the menu. Food was tasty and hot. Definitely will be back.

reviews.name: Phil Blaze
reviews.text: Food was better than average, place is beautiful. After dinner we walked to bar to finish our drinks. It was the owner's birthday and the bartender asked if we wanted a shot of tequila with her for her birthday. Not wanting another drink, but being polite, we said sure. We say Happy Birthday, do our shots, and next thing you know we received a bill for $50 for 2 shots! It's not even the money, it was the way it was handled. Shaddy!

reviews.name: _MARTiN
reviews.text: Great atmosphere, great service, great food, will definitely recommend. Great atmosphere, great service, great food, will definitely recommend.

reviews.name: Fabby Galicia
reviews.text: The food was amazing, the best chilaquiles I’ve ever had! Everything tasted authentic and fresh. My licuado was delicious, the tamalitos were incredible, and the café de olla was so good. The service really stood out because they even made something for us that wasn’t on the menu without hesitation. Highly recommend!

reviews.name: Ryan Bruns
reviews.text: Daniel is the best server! Would definitely come back just because of him

reviews.name: Charissa Lynn
reviews.text: Our first time here since the renovations! It's beautiful, bright and as always, delicious! We had the pleasure of being here for a birthday party. The staff was exceptional, and we thoroughly enjoyed our experience! Thank you for your hospitality!

reviews.name: Claudia Dominguez
reviews.text: Excellent service! We went to celebrate our friend's birthday; we were a group of 15. The waitresses Hanna and Andrea were very friendly and recommended the best options. The managers, Miguel and Rene, were very professional, and we loved the details on the tables! We will definitely return to this place; the food was delicious.

reviews.name: Scott Hansen
reviews.text: Wonderful new atmosphere. Food was wonderful. Drinks were fun

reviews.name: Nadezhda Mead
reviews.text: "What an amazing service from Daniel who offered us to try a small app of ceviche, what can I cay: Q rico!!! Followed by best cocktails from Miguel, who is an absolute doll! The food is incredible, I had fajitas, sissy had ceviche that is to die for! We will absolutely be back soon with friends!"

reviews.name: A. B.
reviews.text: One of the best places to enjoy food, drinks, and great service. Daniel and Miguel(bartender) are fantastic! If you want to enjoy good food and drinks bring some friends and get ready for a great time.

reviews.name: Joseph D. Ruiz
reviews.text: Anna was our lovely waitress today Feb 16th we had a good variety of of entrée and all were absolutely amazing full stop this is authentic cuisine from start to finish we will come back and often is certain when a place is this good no sorry GREAT is better said we will look forward to seeing Anna and try some specialties like pozole is my next item. JDR

reviews.name: Jay Krebs
reviews.text: It’s not what it used to be as far as Mexican dishes - they lost the recipes. You can eat it but there are now better Mexican restaurants around.

reviews.name: Larry Turzynski
reviews.text: Newly remodeled! Excellent queso fundido, burritos and margaritas!

reviews.name: Stela Levario
reviews.text: Great food good experience food was hot & delicious there was plenty of parking friendly staff i would definitely recommend El Molino.

reviews.name: Mike Gell
reviews.text: How to retried after the remodel. Used to go there a lot always had good food It's all nice and white inside makes a very roomy and open Food was awesome service was great . Hemisphere very good.

reviews.name: Maria Aguilar
reviews.text: We have been at the Molino Mexican twice and we were very satisfied with the place, service food even though they just open everything thing was Exquisite 👌

reviews.name: JP
reviews.text: Very friendly staff and the food is amazing. I would definitely come back again soon! Our server, Daniel was very sweet and attentive. What a cozy and beautiful restaurant.

reviews.name: Mike Maloni
reviews.text: Their green salsa is amazingand their service is amazing, highly recommend

reviews.name: Jeremy Alward
reviews.text: "Edit for new 2026 owners: Food is still tasty with impressive portions for the price. I ordered a Chimichanga and it was almost all meat. Drinks are also large and tasty. But this place has management issues. We were in the back room, and there was only 1 waiter for seven tables (40+ customers, I counted). They are clearly understaffed, and the back room should not have been open. Instead all the tables suffered with slow services, the kitchen was swamped, and two of our four orders came out wrong. This place is just two or three small changes away from being great."

reviews.name: Kim V
reviews.text: Loved the new El Molino! The staff is so upscale, love the professional look of all the staff. Food presentation was so pleasant. Margaritas not all lime juice. Owner greeted us, very nice , made you feel welcome. The owner really wanted to please her guests. You can clearly see pride in ownership. Very impressed, been going to El molinos for 40 years. What a nice change.

reviews.name: Karley Swetz
reviews.text: Absolutely loved our dinner and night at the new El Molino. The owner and her staff are lovely. Give it a try!

reviews.name: Cendy Delgadillo
reviews.text: We waited a long time. The food was delicious. I understand they're starting up. The waiter promised to serve coffee, but it never arrived. When I received my bill, I was charged for two coffees that weren't actually coffee.

reviews.name: Aaron Gentry
reviews.text: Food was good. 3 dollars for a small amount of sour cream. 3% service charge and we paid cash.

reviews.name: kenneth nelson
reviews.text: Should have left it alone. Very disappointing. Food used to be some of the best real Mexican food in the area, now basically flavorless mush. Service was mediocre at best, only one other table seated. Sad to see the revamp of a once great restaurant, into a second rate place. I will not return.

reviews.name: Thomas Oconnell
reviews.text: The new el molino is great if ur looking for a delicious meal. We've been going here for years and the food is better than ever. Enchiladas,queso fundito, and quesadillas w machaca beef were some of the best we ever had. The strawberry margaritas were really nice as usual, not as sweet but a little boozier so definitely no complaints. The inside atmosphere is more modern than authentic mexican which i wasn't crazy about but it looks very bright and clean.

reviews.name: Lynn E
reviews.text: El Molino just reopened after a big decorative revamp. I think the service issues we experienced were likely due to wait staff inexperience and the big crowd. Their parking lot was inadequate for the size of the restaurant. We found one of the few spots and were quickly seated in a requested booth and several tables were also available. When we left, 2 cars were blocking us and and gratefully moved so 1 could get our spot. Service was friendly but slow. Staff was good about volunteering more chips and salsa. A request for a sauce to be served on the side never appeared and our margaritas and a refill were very slow. The food was a highlight. The tortilla chips were heavy, like the ones in Aculpolco and the green and red salsas were terrific. I had comarones veracruzanos which said it was served on a bed a rice but the rice was on the side. It was a generous portion (10 large shrimp with tails removed) but with all the tasty chips and slow delivery, I ended up taking some home. The margaritas were great and big (only size option was huge or a pitcher-- big and small servings would be good--we split the second one and wouldn't have needed it if service were faster). They had a unique flavor we loved. We'll be back after a few months so they have time to iron out the wrinkles.

reviews.name: Vipin Patel
reviews.text: nice TASTE IN FOOD

reviews.name: Karla Garcia
reviews.text: We came to try the soft opening of their breakfast and everything was absolutely fantastic. We got cafes de olla, birria, menudo, and chilaquiles. Everything was full of flavor and delicious. The staff was all super friendly and the restaurant is beautiful. 10/10 would recommend!!! The new owners are amazing.

reviews.name: robert nausid
reviews.text: Just found out yesterday that our favorite Mexican restaurant just reopened. We figured out that it has new owners so we wanted to try it. We were not disappointed the renovations the new owners did were excellent. They made the restaurant more open and brighter you can actually see the food your are consuming. Well we both ordered the enchillada suizas i had cheese and my wife had chicken they were excellant was not disapointed at all. There is only one thing that kinda turned me off but it doesn't mean that we will not be returning the cost . i thought $12.00 for an order of quac was a little high and our meals were a little on the pricey side but the food sure was good. We will be going back

reviews.name: Mike Fitch
reviews.text: El Molino a staple of the community for many years is reopening and better than ever. New recipes and old recipes are back again. Great atmosphere and great food.

reviews.name: Victor Flores
reviews.text: I have to start by saying that the food is good, the service starts off very well until one of the managers served me and it wasn't so good... There was one detail I noticed that, for me, made the difference between giving 4 stars and only one... One of the managers, who yesterday, December 26th, was wearing a burgundy sweater and had very short hair, went to clean a table and, while removing the dirty glasses, put his fingers in them, which is unhygienic. He also touched clean plates that were on the busboy, and I thought that was very wrong since I'm a manager in a restaurant and that's not allowed by the city... I think they should train the managers better.

reviews.name: Daniel Peabody
reviews.text: Its under new ownership, they have updated the look inside, but the menu seems to be the same. This was always one of our favorite restaurants, and I hope they stay open for a lot time!

reviews.name: S B
reviews.text: $20 plate for a tiny wing, drum stick and thigh. Portions get smaller Charged $3 for the extra mole sauce because I wanted to take home so I can put over chicken breast. The mole sauce was delicious, only wish they would serve with chicken breast or a decent size chicken thigh. The margaritas are made with Sour Mix. So be sure to ask "Traditional" for a fresh made one, Absolutely delicious.

reviews.name: Aleida Rivas
reviews.text: It’s a very modern with authentic Mexican style food. We had tacos de carnitas and very delicious everything was worth it with fresh ingredients. Can’t wait to go again!

reviews.name: Irais Collazo
reviews.text: I think the server was Carolina but if not whatever server was servering us as a party of 20 people on 12/19/2015 are experience was amazing. She was fast. She was organized, she did not Miss A beat. She did not miss an order. And when it came to pay out, everything was correct in every single way If her name is caroline , she deserves a race or at less a bonus , especially in this holiday time , she was absolutely amazing thank you so much girl you did grate we do this with our family once a year and I'm a 100%.Sure will be coming back to this restaurant :)

reviews.name: Terri Dour
reviews.text: Ordered to tamales to go. Live within 5 minutes of the restaurant- food was cold and not good. Only ordered the tamales - they tasted like they had been frozen and reheated. The salsa roja was more sweet and not at all spicy. So disappointed- will not try this place again for any other food.

reviews.name: S B
reviews.text: My husband picked us up some salsa and chips since new management took over. Two 16 oz containers of salsa and a “bag” of chips (I counted about 25) and the bill was $30. I had to pick my jaw up off the floor. I told him that was insane. Apparently with the new management comes new inflated pricing.

reviews.name: Mary S.
reviews.text: My boyfriend and I shared the chicken fajitas, and I had a steak taco. The food was all very tasty! Great atmosphere. We will be back.

reviews.name: Kathy Roger
reviews.text: Couldn't wait to try after remodel. Very nice inside . Service was great and attentive . Food was good. I ordered something I dont normally order but want to go back and have Machaca beef . My husband had a burrito and he ate it all. My sister had Asada tacos and also liked hers . They did not have the fried ice cream which was disappointing. We had the cherry dessert was a but hard would not get that again. Overall nice place. Tuesdays and Thursdays they have $9 margaritas. Check first before ordering.

reviews.name: jorge mendoza
reviews.text: Mmm not very happy with the food seems like they're slacking on it. the food isn't how I once had it. They're use to be more sauce ,more flavor and more quantity.

reviews.name: chelsea Stuckemeyer
reviews.text: Daniel gave amazing service tonight. Food was one of the best quality I have seen in years. Margarita was on point!!

reviews.name: lauren hope
reviews.text: Thank you to the new owner and the staff! We had a wonderful time tonight celebrating our wedding! This has been our favorite restaurant for years and are so happy they are open again! We will be back soon! They really out did themselves with making our night special! Thank you again!

reviews.name: Denten Laura
reviews.text: Food was terrible. The guacamole was bland and the “fried ice cream’ was a scoop of vanilla with something cirnflajes-like sprinkled over, chocolate syrup drizzled and all in a waffle-type bowl that can be purchased at Jewel, Aldi etc.

reviews.name: Juan Munoz
reviews.text: Decided to try this restaurant after it had been sold to new owners. It's very overpriced , service is not the best and food is bland. Will not be going back.

reviews.name: Destiny Clay
reviews.text: food is beyond amazing and came very fast

reviews.name: Luke Skywalker
reviews.text: Outstanding food & service

reviews.name: Krystle Rederer
reviews.text: Love this place. Would go here to drink their sweet green salsa alone! Gets a bit noisy during their busy time. Only complaint I have (BUT THEY ARE CURRENTLY REMODELING SO MAY NOT BE THE CASE ANYMORE!!!) was that it was a bit cramped at the tables during rush times and poor ventillation, so I've been avoiding going since i started getting hot flashes.

reviews.name: Brenda Elizarraraz
reviews.text: This place needs to be closed down. Food is not authentic at all.

reviews.name: On-Call Service McNally's Heating & Cooling
reviews.text: Best Mexican food around and definitely best salsas!

reviews.name: Sheila Alder
reviews.text: This has been our favorite place to go for 15 years! Authentic Mexican food and huge portions, we always have some to take home. Their Margaritas are AMAZING! Highly recommend!

reviews.name: DON ANDGONE
reviews.text: I used to get takeout from this place years ago, and I was in the area and decided to revisit them. I spoke highly of the place and had to apologize for the disappointing service we received. The server was very dry and seemed to not want to be working. He wasn't rude, but he was not very friendly or helpful. We should've never had to ask if we could order something to drink. Once I was able to place a drink order, I was told they were out of the juice they needed to make it. It was the drink special for the weekend, so I'm guessing it did very well. Plus, it saddens me to say that the food was just ok. I really used to like the food I would get from this place. The shrimp fajita were not very flavorful, and neither was the salsa that came with the chips. I asked if they had any other sauces or salsa, and I'm pleased to say that they have a chipotle based sauce that was very delicious and flavorful. All in all, the service was worse than the food, and the meal I received was just ok. Thanks for the memories, El Molino's, but I think I'll just move on. Hopefully, things will get better in the future.

reviews.name: Sean OBrien
reviews.text: Great traditional Mexican food.

reviews.name: William Cohn
reviews.text: We returned for dinner and I had the El Molino combo plate, Skirt Stake, Chicken Taco, and Cheese Enchilada. It was tasty and a great portion. We will be back.

reviews.name: Lee Orenstein
reviews.text: Terrific all around!

reviews.name: Aaron McCracken
reviews.text: Good food and service.

reviews.name: Dora McCracken
reviews.text: Love the stuff jalapeños.

reviews.name: john pencak
reviews.text: "Have been going here since 2005. My favorite Mexican restaurant by far. There green salad with chips. The beans I think are the best. I always order a small play of beans with Chihuahua cheese melt on top for an appetizer. My go to is::::: The #2 combination is the combination. Chicken taco, enchilada, Skirt steak... 👍"

reviews.name: Jason Ruggiero
reviews.text: Amazing food and fantastic service! I recently dined at El Molino and it was an outstanding experience. My family and I were welcomed when we arrived and seated quickly even though they were very busy. The food was very good. The Chiles Rellenos and the Carne Oaxaquena were incredible. Our server was quick and friendly as well, always checking on us to make sure we had full drinks for us and the kids. If you’re craving authentic Mexican food with great service, you have to check this place out! Highly recommended!

reviews.name: Witch
reviews.text: Excellent food and staff 🤞 …

reviews.name: Regina Muscat
reviews.text: This is the first Mexican Restaurant I went to in the early ‘80s. Even with so many competitors popping up , they are still the best around ! Thank you staff and management

reviews.name: Olik Maharg
reviews.text: El Molino Mexican Restaurant is a charming spot with a relaxed vibe, perfect for enjoying a few drinks and some delicious tacos. The food is consistently good, and the atmosphere makes it an enjoyable place to unwind. The building itself is beautifully decorated, both inside and out, with flowers and other decorative touches that really give the place character. It has a laid-back, inviting feel that makes you want to hang out a little longer. The staff is always friendly and helpful, especially when it comes to choosing from their menu. They offer great suggestions and make sure you're comfortable with your order, which I really appreciate. I usually go when it's not too busy, so I can only speak to the service during those quieter times, but it’s always been quick and attentive. Overall, El Molino is a great spot for a casual meal and drinks, and I find myself going there every now and then when I’m in the mood for good Mexican food in a chilled-out setting.

reviews.name: Eric Grawe
reviews.text: Chicken chimichanga delite is fantastic!!! Will definitely be coming back! Red salsa is hot, beware!

reviews.name: Lily Zepeda
reviews.text: Love this place. I’ve been coming here for almost the 40 years they’ve been open.

reviews.name: Vicki Madigan
reviews.text: Great food and service. Indoor temperature could have been cooler, less humid.

reviews.name: Juan Perry
reviews.text: I wish I had taken a picture of my burrito. It was smaller than my tacos. Not to mention it didn't have anyone in it. It was only meat. I didn't know I needed to add the toppings. The food was not tasty. Long drive for no reason. Not to mention we kept having to repeat everything we wanted. Taco Bell would have been better.

reviews.name: Anthony DiFiore
reviews.text: On a scale of 1-10 on food, 7. Service. 2. Had to ask for water 3 times in 30 min. Server, no personality. No contact at all. Didn't ask if food was ok, did we need anything, something to drink? We had to bring our receipt inside to be checked out bc our server disappeared!!! Will not return.

reviews.name: Anthony Fata
reviews.text: I had the Guadalajara combo meal. It was a large amount, and extremely delicious. The margaritas are good, particularly the spicy cucumber jalapeno margarita. Vaguely Familiar, the band that plays on Thursdays is excellent. They really rock with acoustic renditions of popular classic rock songs.

reviews.name: Timothy Christians
reviews.text: This place is a hidden gem, the kind you'd find on the food networks hidden local spots. It's easy to drive by without noticing if you're from out of the area, but don't be fooled. This place is legitimately the best food. When I lived down 25 from here id stop here probably once a month it's just that damn good.

reviews.name: battnotinclud
reviews.text: Service is quick, portion sizes are respectable, has indoor and outdoor seating, has a sizeable bar and friendly staff.

reviews.name: Steven H
reviews.text: Food was great

reviews.name: Michele Fuller
reviews.text: Great food. Nice atmosphere. Very enjoyable experience.

reviews.name: Robert Kosin
reviews.text: Need to look twice that this restaurant is celebrating 40 years at this location when service and kitchen has not missed a mark in that time. Comments are well deserved about the quality of margaritas but it the kitchen that carries the dinning experience. If there is a need for refresh, it’s a decoration here or there and maybe that just confirms this is the place of the last tasty dining experience and not disappointed on the revisit again. Congratulations el Molino.

reviews.name: Tom Hege
reviews.text: We have been coming here for over 20 years! Best strawberry margaritas, appetizers and combo plates...

reviews.name: Rafael Nuno
reviews.text: Loved the food and service

reviews.name: Jeremy Sturgis
reviews.text: Good food and service as always

reviews.name: Sandy
reviews.text: The food is always excellent even during the pandemic we did take out superb!
